section-1.title: Public sector duty regarding socio-economic inequalities

section-1-1.content: An authority to which this section applies must, when making decisions of a strategic nature about how to exercise its functions, have due regard to the desirability of exercising them in a way that is designed to reduce the inequalities of outcome which result from socio-economic disadvantage.

section-1-2.content: In deciding how to fulfil a duty to which it is subject under subsection (1), an authority must take into account any guidance issuedin accordance with subsection (2A).

section-1-2A.content: The guidance to be taken into account under subsection (2) is—

section-1-3.content: The authorities to which this section applies are—

section-1-3-b.content: a government department other than the Security Service, the Secret Intelligence Service or the Government Communications Head-quarters;

section-1-3-ba.content: a corporate joint committee established by regulations made under Part 5 of the Local Government and Elections (Wales) Act 2021;

section-1-3-c.content: a county council or district council in England;

section-1-3-f.content: the Common Council of the City of London in its capacity as a local authority;

section-1-3-g.content: the Council of the Isles of Scilly;

section-1-3-k.content: apolice and crime commissionerestablished for an area in England.

section-1-3-cn1.content: Keeper of the Registers of Scotland;

section-1-3-gn1.content: a council constituted under section 2 of the Local Governmentetc.(Scotland) Act 1994;

section-1-3-hn1.content: an integration joint board established under section 9(2) of the Public Bodies (Joint Working) (Scotland) Act 2014;

section-1-3-in1.content: a Health Board constituted under section 2(1)(a) of the National Health Service (Scotland) Act 1978;

section-1-3-jn1.content: a Special Health Board constituted under section 2(1)(b) of that Act;

section-1-3A.content: This section also applies to the following authorities—

section-1-6.content: The reference to inequalities in subsection (1) does not include any inequalities experienced by a person as a result of being a person subject to immigration control within the meaning given by section 115(9) of the Immigration and Asylum Act 1999.

section-2-1.content: A Minister of the Crown may by regulations amend section 1 so as to—

section-2-1-a.content: add a public authority to the authorities that are subject to the duty under subsection (1) of that section;

section-2-1-b.content: remove an authority from those that are subject to the duty;

section-2-1-c.content: make the duty apply, in the case of a particular authority, only in relation to certain functions that it has;

section-2-1-d.content: in the case of an authority to which the application of the duty is already restricted to certain functions, remove or alter the restriction.

section-2-2.content: In subsection (1) “public authority” means an authority that has functions of a public nature.

section-2-3.content: Provision made under subsection (1) may not impose a duty on an authority in relation to any devolved Scottish functions or devolved Welsh functions.

section-2-4.content: The Scottish Ministers or the Welsh Ministers may by regulations amend section 1 so as to—

section-2-4-a.content: add a relevant authority to the authorities that are subject to the duty under subsection (1) of that section;

section-2-4-b.content: remove a relevant authority from those that are subject to the duty;

section-2-4-c.content: make the duty apply, in the case of a particular relevant authority, only in relation to certain functions that it has;

section-2-4-d.content: in the case of a relevant authority to which the application of the duty is already restricted to certain functions, remove or alter the restriction.

section-2-5.content: For the purposes of the power conferred by subsection (4) on the Scottish Ministers, “relevant authority” means an authority whose functions—

section-2-5-a.content: are exercisable only in or as regards Scotland,

section-2-5-b.content: are wholly or mainly devolved Scottish functions, and

section-2-5-c.content: correspond or are similar to those of an authority for the time being specified in section 1(3).

section-2-6.content: For the purposes of the power conferred by subsection (4) on the Welsh Ministers, “relevant authority” meansa devolved Welsh authority (within the meaning given by section 157A of the Government of Wales Act 2006) whose functions correspondor are similar to those of an authority for the time being specified in subsection (3) of section 1 or referred to in subsection (4) of that section.

section-2-8.content: Regulations under this section may make any amendments of section 1 that appear to the Minister or Ministers to be necessary or expedient in consequence of provision made under subsection (1) or (as the case may be) subsection (4).

section-2-11-a.content: a function is a devolved Scottish function if it is exercisable in or as regards Scotland and it does not relate to reserved matters (within the meaning of the Scotland Act 1998);

section-3.title: Enforcement
section-3.content: A failure in respect of a performance of a duty under section 1 does not confer a cause of action at private law.
